skill.name: ambiguous-question-guide
skill.description: Identifies ambiguous government service questions, detects missing subjects, license names, or process steps, and generates one precise clarification question.
skill.summary: Clarifies incomplete public service questions with one targeted follow-up.
skill.author: dxy-hub

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command matches and high-entropy warnings, but review found Markdown code fences, inline examples, and Chinese instructional text rather than executable code. No prompt-injection attempt, network behavior, filesystem access, credential handling, or malicious intent was found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Matches Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick locations are Markdown fences and inline examples used to show expected clarification questions and numeric labels. They do not invoke a shell, run scripts, or process user-controlled command input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are visible Markdown code blocks containing Chinese sample outputs and flow diagrams. No executable language syntax, shell invocation, or command runner is present at these locations.

low_findings.title: High Entropy Warnings Are Natural Language Content
low_findings.description: The high-entropy warnings point to Markdown files containing dense Chinese text, tables, and examples. Review found readable documentation, not packed binaries, encrypted payloads, or obfuscated executable content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files render as plain Markdown with headings and human-readable Chinese guidance. No encoded blob, binary data, or decode-and-execute pattern was observed.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Pattern Is A Scanner Misclassification
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm finding at SKILL.md line 3 occurs in the skill description text. Review found a Chinese description of ambiguity detection, not a cryptographic API or hash implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is frontmatter description text about government service question handling. There is no MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other cryptographic operation on that line.

content.value_statement: Public service users often ask incomplete questions that cannot map to a specific procedure. This skill identifies the missing element and produces one precise follow-up question.
actual_capabilities: Classifies public service questions as ambiguous or ready for processing.
Detects missing license names, subject types, business names, and process steps.
Prioritizes the most important missing element before asking a follow-up.
Generates one concise and polite clarification question in Chinese.
Uses examples for permits, subsidies, certificates, registration, and household scenarios.
Provides rules for avoiding repeated, generic, or multi-part questions.
limitations: It does not retrieve live government procedures or policy documents.
It assumes Chinese public service terminology and examples.
It does not verify legal eligibility or document requirements.
It may need local policy customization for a specific agency or region.

use_cases.title: Improve Service Chatbot Triage
use_cases.description: Route vague citizen questions toward a specific permit, certificate, benefit, or registration workflow.

use_cases.title: Standardize Agent Follow-Ups
use_cases.description: Give service desk staff consistent wording for one focused clarification question.

use_cases.title: Evaluate Query Readiness
use_cases.description: Check whether a user question contains enough detail before sending it to a search or workflow system.

prompt_templates.title: Classify a Single Question
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner check for one user message.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to decide whether this public service question is ambiguous: "{{user_question}}". If it is ambiguous, ask one precise follow-up question.

prompt_templates.title: Find the Missing Element
prompt_templates.scenario: Identify why a service question cannot be processed yet.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this question: "{{user_question}}". Identify the missing element, choose the highest priority missing element, and return one polite clarification question.

prompt_templates.title: Rewrite a Generic Follow-Up
prompt_templates.scenario: Replace vague support language with a targeted question.
prompt_templates.prompt: The current reply is "{{current_reply}}" for the user question "{{user_question}}". Rewrite it as one specific public service clarification question.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Batch of Service Queries
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of multiple chatbot inputs.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these public service questions: {{question_list}}. For each item, mark whether it is ready for processing or needs one clarification question.

output_examples.input: User asks: "I want to apply for a permit."
output_examples.output: The question is ambiguous because the permit type is missing. The response asks which permit type the user needs, with relevant examples.

output_examples.input: User asks: "What materials are needed for a business license?"
output_examples.output: The question needs clarification about the applicant type. The response asks whether the user is applying as an individual business owner or an enterprise.
The service is specific, but the processing step is missing. The response asks whether the user is applying for the first time, changing, or renewing.

best_practices: Ask only one follow-up question for each ambiguous user request.
Prioritize missing license or business names before applicant type or process step.
Keep examples relevant to the service category named by the user.
anti_patterns: Do not ask several clarification questions in one response.
Do not use generic replies such as asking the user to provide more details.
Do not classify a vague scenario as ready when several services could match it.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It judges whether a public service question is specific enough and creates one targeted clarification question when needed.

faq.question: Which language is it designed for?
faq.answer: It is designed for Chinese public service conversations and uses Chinese examples and response wording.

faq.question: Does it answer policy questions directly?
faq.answer: No. It prepares the question for routing or search by resolving missing information first.

faq.question: Can it support permits and certificates?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes patterns for permits, certificates, business licenses, registration, subsidies, and related services.

faq.question: Does it need live data access?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a rules and template guide and does not require network or database access.

faq.question: Can agencies customize the examples?
faq.answer: Yes. Agencies should adapt examples to local service catalogs and official terminology.
